WaPageTitle.value: Rolex Awards - A self-made environmentalist in India - Video

metadescription.value: An unorthodox environmentalist, Romulus Whitaker has made it his life's mission to protect rainforests in India. Watch the video on rolex.org.

previewTitle.value: Rolex Awards - A self-made environmentalist in India - Video

text.value: Romulus Whitaker turned a lifelong interest in reptiles, especially the king cobra, into a six decades’ long career of protecting the environment in his adoptive country of India. The 2008 Rolex Awards for Enterprise Laureate established the Agumbe Rainforest Research Station in India’s Western Ghats mountain range in 2005, which has since become a base for research on rainforest ecology and water conservation. With his Award funding he founded three other research stations across the country. He was also instrumental in initiating a number of NGOs concerned with saving the natural world − from snakes to crocodiles.

pageCaption.value: Brazilian environmentalist José Márcio Ayres, who died in March 2003, devoted his life to the challenge of combining protection of the Amazon forest with improving the living conditions of its native people.

metadescription.value: Brazilian environmentalist José Márcio Ayres, who died in March 2003, devoted his life to the challenge of combining protection of the Amazon forest with improving the living conditions of its native people.

pageCaption.value: American Christine Keung is using her college education as a force for good by tackling rural pollution in northwest China, where her father was sent during the Cultural Revolution. Her ambition is to help authorities find long-term solutions to hazardous waste, and women – who bear the disproportionate cost of environmental degradation as men migrate to cities for work – are the key to her project.

pageTitle.value: Rolex Awards Video - A vanished forest reborn

pageCaption.value: Working with dedicated farming families, Laury Cullen Jr. is restoring Brazil’s Atlantic Forest while contributing to the local economy and helping to fight climate change.

metadescription.value: Laury Cullen Jr.’s Dream Map initiative has overseen the planting of four million trees in Brazil’s Atlantic Forest. Watch the video on rolex.org.

pageTitle.value: Rolex Awards - Protecting waterbodies in India  - Video

pageCaption.value: Determined from an early age to revive and protect the waterbodies near his home in Chennai, 2012 Rolex Awards for Enterprise Laureate Arun Krishnamurthy gave up a promising career at Google to devote his life to rejuvenating the lakes in his native India and beyond.

WaPageTitle.value: Rolex Awards Video - Reviving India’s blighted lakes

metadescription.value: In 2021, Arun Krishnamurthy united 10,000 volunteers in his mission to restore and protect dying waterbodies in India. Watch the video on rolex.org

pageTitle.value: Promote local initiatives for biodiversity conservation in Nepal’s Trans-Himalaya - Video

pageCaption.value: Local people from one of the world’s wildest and most isolated places, the mountainous Himalaya region of Humla in Nepal, are being enlisted as frontline conservators to rescue dwindling wild animal populations – from snow leopards to wild yaks. Driving the scheme is an energetic young ecologist, Rinzin Phunjok Lama, who is convinced that only local commitment and know-how can make the real difference.

WaPageTitle.value: Promote local initiatives for biodiversity conservation in Nepal’s Trans-Himalaya - Video

metadescription.value: Rinzin Phunjok Lama passionately recounts his project  that is helping to save the rich biodiversity in the trans-Himalayas. See more on rolex.org.
